name: Brazilian Center of Analysis and Planning (CEBRAP)
current_projects: International Postdoctoral Program (IPP); Mecila (Maria Sibylla Merian Centre for Conviviality-Inequality in Latin America); COMPLUS public health governance research; DesJus seminars on inequality and justice; cebrap.lab methodological training program
funding_sources: BNDES, FAPESP, CNPq, IDRC, Elizabeth Blackwell Institute (UK), international research partnerships
notes: CEBRAP is an independent, non-university-affiliated social science research center founded in 1969 in Sao Paulo. It spans sociology, political science, anthropology, urban studies, philosophy, and law.

name: Center for Studies on Economic Development (CEDE)
current_projects: Observatorio de coyuntura económica y social (Economic and Social Observatory); applied research in competitiveness, institutional economics, and economic history; consulting projects for Colombian public sector institutions.
funding_sources: Domestic: Banco de la República, Colombian ministries (Finance, Education, Health). International: Open Society Foundation, Ford Foundation, NBER, IDB, World Bank, NSF.
notes: One of Colombia's oldest and most prominent economic research centers, with approximately 40 full-time professors managing dozens of projects annually.

name: Pathways Beyond Neoliberalism: Voices from MENA
institution: American University in Cairo, School of Global Affairs and Public Policy
current_projects: Annual PEMENA Conference; spring/summer school for PhD candidates and early-career researchers; oral history documentation of trade policy; Samer Soliman Book Award; Political Economy of the Global South Conference (PEGS)
notes: Part of a broader Ford Foundation-funded global initiative titled 'Responding to the Neoliberal Economic Model: Voices from the Global South,' launched October 2022.

name: New Political Economy Initiative (NPEI)
current_projects: Four research clusters: (1) Industrial Policy and Structural Transformation, (2) Regional Political Economy, (3) Social Structures of the Economy, (4) Public Goods and Public Sphere. Annual conference: Economy and Society in Contemporary India.
funding_sources: Ford Foundation (approximately $4 million / Rs 35 crore grant)
connected_networks: Beyond Neoliberalism Network (University of Cambridge / Queen Mary University of London)
notes: Housed within the Centre for Liberal Education (CLE) at IIT Bombay. Director Anush Kapadia is Associate Professor in Humanities and Social Sciences.

name: Program for the Economic Analysis of Mexico (Programa de Economia Aplicada)
current_projects: Reimaginar el capitalismo (Reimagining Capitalism): a collective, multidisciplinary international project examining relationships between economy, government, and society.
funding_sources: Primarily institutionally funded through El Colegio de México.
connected_networks: Centro de Estudios Económicos (CEE) at El Colegio de México
notes: Formally known as PREA, housed within the Centro de Estudios Económicos at El Colegio de México.

name: Southern Centre for Inequality Studies (SCIS)
current_projects: African Green Resources, Energy and Economy (AGREE) examining Africa's role in the global green transition through critical minerals; African Care and Climate Innovation Alliance (ACACIA) Hub.
funding_sources: Open Society Foundations; Ford Foundation; Presidential Climate Commission partnerships
notes: Interdisciplinary research centre focused on understanding and addressing inequality in the global South. Director Imraan Valodia also serves as Pro Vice-Chancellor for Climate, Sustainability and Inequality at Wits.

name: Reimagining Cohesive Capitalism
institution: London School of Economics and Political Science (STICERD)
director: Professor Sir Tim Besley and Daniel Chandler
current_projects: Research on post-neoliberal economic models; public engagement seminars and workshops; 10-week curriculum on the future of capitalism run through LSE School of Public Policy.
funding_sources: Open Society Foundations (Ideas and Fellowships Collaborative)
notes: Programme hosted within STICERD and the LSE Department of Economics.

name: Institute for Innovation and Public Purpose (IIPP)
current_projects: Mission-Aligned World Bank: From Ambition to Delivery; Reclaiming Digital Sovereignty; Macrofinancial Policy for a Green and Just Transition.
funding_sources: UCL; funded research projects with the World Bank and international policymakers.
connected_networks: UCL Bartlett Faculty of the Built Environment
global policymaker network spanning economics, architecture, arts, and design
notes: Founded and directed by economist Mariana Mazzucato, known for the concept of the entrepreneurial state. Located at 11 Montague Street, London WC1B 5BP.

name: Columbia Center for Political Economy
current_projects: Research on political determinants of economic policy; comparative studies of labor market institutions; political economy of inequality and redistribution.
funding_sources: Columbia University; NSF and federal research grants; private foundations.
connected_networks: Columbia School of International and Public Affairs (SIPA)
Saltzman Institute of War and Peace Studies
notes: Brings together economists and political scientists at Columbia University to study the interaction between political institutions and economic outcomes.

name: Reimagining the Economy Initiative
director: Dani Rodrik and Gordon Hanson (Faculty Co-Directors); Rohan Sandhu (Executive Director)
current_projects: Local economic development data collection (domestic and global); podcast series on practitioner knowledge; research on regional joblessness, housing costs, and intergenerational mobility; Global Economic Transformation Initiative launch.
funding_sources: $7.5 million grant from the William and Flora Hewlett Foundation; Omidyar Network; total cross-institution commitment exceeds $40 million.
connected_networks: Malcolm Wiener Center for Social Policy
notes: Housed within the Malcolm Wiener Center for Social Policy at HKS. Part of a broader Hewlett Foundation and Omidyar Network effort.

name: Center for Equitable Economy and Sustainable Society
current_projects: National clearinghouse of big data on racial and economic disparities; Social Medicine Committee addressing social and historical determinants of health; research on residential segregation and mental health care deserts; convening HBCUs on contemporary economic issues.
funding_sources: William and Flora Hewlett Foundation; Omidyar Network; Ford Foundation; Open Society Foundations
connected_networks: Harvard Kennedy School political economy center
Johns Hopkins University political economy center
notes: Known internally as e2s2. Michael Ralph is Cameron Schrier Professor and Chair of Afro-American Studies; Jevay Grooms is Associate Professor of Economics and NBER Faculty Research Fellow.

name: Center on Economy and Society
current_projects: enCOREage: a free open-access introductory economics e-textbook. AI and the Economy: research into how artificial intelligence is redefining economic landscapes. BA in Moral and Political Economy: undergraduate program.
funding_sources: William and Flora Hewlett Foundation ($10 million); Omidyar Network ($10 million shared); Johns Hopkins University; Stavros Niarchos Foundation (via SNF Agora Institute endowment)
opportunities: Undergraduate and graduate research opportunities through the Moral and Political Economy major; visiting scholar positions.
connected_networks: SNF Agora Institute at Johns Hopkins University
Johns Hopkins Data Science and AI Institute
JHU Krieger School of Arts and Sciences
notes: Housed within the SNF Agora Institute. Advisory board chaired by Margaret Levi (Stanford). Faculty includes Angus Burgin (History), Ling Chen (Political Economy), Henry Farrell (International Affairs).

name: Work of the Future
current_projects: Generative AI Working Group examining how AI design can support higher-quality jobs; Automation Clinic applied research on technology deployment and worker impacts; industrial robotics and emerging technologies research.
funding_sources: MIT Industrial Performance Center; industry and policy partners through working groups.
connected_networks: MIT Department of Aeronautics and Astronautics
notes: Originally launched as the Work of the Future Task Force in 2018; transitioned to an ongoing initiative at MIT's Industrial Performance Center. Key faculty affiliates include Paul Osterman and Nathan Wilmers.

name: Emergent Political Economies
current_projects: A five-year research initiative with workshops and working groups exploring: how technologies develop and spread, urbanization dynamics, banking credit cycles, immigration, and intersections of arts and scientific perspectives on economic systems.
funding_sources: Omidyar Network (primary funder of the five-year theme)
notes: Five-year research theme examining how contemporary global economic systems generate emergent patterns that outpace traditional political and theoretical frameworks.

name: Berkeley Economy and Society Initiative (BESI)
current_projects: White paper series on California affordability and housing; research on federal climate policy effectiveness; studies on financial democracy and neoliberalism's origins; analysis of right-wing populism and U.S. political-economic geography.
funding_sources: William and Flora Hewlett Foundation; philanthropic donor contributions
opportunities: Graduate funding through Designated Emphasis in Political Economy; research stipends and conference funding for graduate students.
connected_networks: Yale ISPS American Political Economy Exchange (APEX)
notes: Co-directors and research leads include Jonas Meckling (Climate) and Marion Fourcade (Technology). Graduate training supported through the Designated Emphasis in Political Economy at UC Berkeley.

name: Center for Critical Imagination
connected_networks: CEBRAP (David Dequech, Senior Professor of Economics at Unicamp, serves as PI connecting both centers)
notes: Director Marcos Nobre is Professor of Political Philosophy at Unicamp (Universidade Estadual de Campinas). David Dequech (Senior Professor of Economics, Unicamp) serves as Principal Investigator with a cross-institutional connection to CEBRAP. Identified from the Wits EPE Network conference participants list (June 2026).
